summary: Genuinely two-dimensional data — rows and columns that mean something together.
use: For datasets where cells relate to both a row and a column header; always with caption and scoped headers.
avoid: Never for layout — that war was won twenty years ago; use CSS grid.

summary: The table's title, announced by screen readers before the data.
use: First child of every table, saying what the dataset is — not how to read it.
avoid: Don't substitute a heading above the table; the caption is programmatically bound to it.

summary: Groups columns for shared attributes and lets rendering start before all rows parse.
use: After caption, with col children spanning the table's columns.
avoid: It styles columns structurally; per-cell semantics still live on th/td.
attributes: span — number of columns when no col children
snippet:
Two grouped columns
ItemQ1Q2

summary: One column (or span of columns) inside a colgroup.
use: To target columns for width or styling without touching every cell.
avoid: Only valid inside colgroup; it holds no content.
attributes: span — how many columns this represents

summary: The block of rows holding the column headers.
use: One per table, before tbody; makes the header row(s) repeat on printed pages and sticky-positionable.
avoid: Header cells inside it still need scope='col' — thead alone doesn't associate them.

summary: The block of rows holding the table's data.
use: Explicitly wrap data rows; multiple tbody elements can group related row batches.
avoid: Browsers insert one implicitly, but writing it keeps the structure honest and styleable.

summary: The block of rows summarizing the table: totals, averages.
use: After tbody, for totals rows; repeats at the bottom of printed pages.
avoid: The summary row's label cell is a th scope='row' like any other.

use: Inside thead, tbody, or tfoot, holding th and td cells.
avoid: Rows belong to row groups; keep data out of the header group.

summary: A header cell that other cells reference for meaning.
use: Always with scope (col or row); in irregular tables give each th an id and point data cells at them with headers.
avoid: A th without scope leaves screen readers guessing which cells it governs.

use: For the values; in complex tables list its governing header ids in the headers attribute.
avoid: If the cell labels other cells rather than holding data, it's a th.
